personas.name: Burnt founder of B2B services SME
personas.icp: Bootstrapped or family-funded founder-CEO of a SG B2B services SME (10–40 headcount, S$1–8M revenue) who hired one of the SG mid-market agencies (Construct Digital, iFoundries, MediaOne, Verz Design) in 2023–25, signed a 6-month minimum, and walked out the door 9 months later with quarterly decks and no leads. The conversation today opens with 'I'm not paying agency fees again unless you can prove ROI monthly'. A stranger names: typical SBF / SCCCI member firm in IT services, accounting, legal, training, logistics, healthtech B2B — the kind of Pte Ltd that has been profitable for 10+ years and now needs to grow beyond the founder's personal network.
pains: Sat through quarterly executive decks with vanity metrics (impressions, reach, brand sentiment) and zero pipeline numbers; cannot get fooled twice
Currently maintains 8+ paid SaaS subscriptions (Mailchimp, Canva, Hootsuite, Semrush, HubSpot Starter, Adobe CC, ChatGPT Plus, Zoom webinars) totalling ~S$650/mo that no one fully uses
Has 1–2 freelancers on Telegram for ad-hoc design / video at S$500–1,200/mo; the founder still ends up writing the briefs at midnight
Doesn't know if the marketing manager who quit last quarter actually drove anything — the in-house Notion was abandoned with no handover
PSG eligibility hurdles confuse the founder — knows the grant exists, doesn't know which vendor is listed, gives up and self-funds
personas.current_workaround: 8+ paid SaaS subscriptions (~S$650/mo) maintained by no one in particular, 1–2 freelancers on Telegram (S$500–1,200/mo) coordinated by the founder herself, ad-hoc agency engagements when a campaign push is needed (S$3–8k one-off, repeated 3–4×/yr), and the founder absorbing brief-writing + reporting + campaign QA personally at ~4 hr/wk.

nba.summary: Steady-state DIY tool stack + 1–2 freelancers + 4 hr/wk founder time on briefs / reporting / vendor coordination. The 'I'll just hire a marketing manager' alternative is benchmarked at ~S$6,456/mo all-in (PayScale SG 2026), which the founder rejects as too rich for a 30-headcount SME — and the ad-hoc agency project work (S$3–8k 3–4×/yr) is excluded from steady-state NBA per the displaced-spend convention.

tooling_lines.tool: 8 paid marketing SaaS subscriptions (HubSpot Starter + Mailchimp + Canva Pro + Hootsuite + Semrush + Adobe CC + ChatGPT Plus + Zoom)
Founder time on briefs / reporting / vendor coordination (4 hr/wk @ S$80/hr loaded)

personas.name: In-house marketer at scale-up needing production extension
personas.icp: Solo marketing manager / head of growth at a SG scale-up (40–120 headcount, S$5–20M revenue, post-Series-A or profitable bootstrapped) drowning in production. The role was hired to drive strategy + brand + GTM but actually spends 70% of the week shipping social posts, ad creatives, blog content, landing-page tweaks, and chasing the freelancer roster. The procurement champion is the in-house marketer; the economic buyer is the founder-CEO or COO. A stranger names: marketing leads at typical Wavemaker / Insignia / Vertex SEA Series-A portfolio companies (Aspire, Volopay, Endowus, Sleek growth cohort, Carro, Glints) where the marketing function exists but is a one-person band.
pains: 70% of the week spent on production (briefs, design QA, post scheduling, ad creative iterations) vs. 30% on actual strategy / GTM / channel decisions
Roster of 4–6 freelancers across copy / design / video / paid-media each with their own brief format, payment terms, and Slack channel — vendor-management overhead is ~10 hr/wk
Cannot scale content velocity without either hiring a junior (12-week recruit cycle, S$4–5k/mo all-in) or burning out trying to ship 20+ assets/mo personally
CEO asks for monthly board-pack ROI reporting but the data lives in 6 dashboards (Meta, Google Ads, GA4, Mailchimp, HubSpot, Hootsuite) and the marketer rebuilds the slide each cycle
Wants to test a new channel (e.g. LinkedIn ads, podcast, B2B newsletter sponsorship) but cannot find the bandwidth to run a 3-month experiment alongside steady-state production
personas.current_workaround: Solo marketing manager (S$5,400–8,000/mo all-in) coordinating 4–6 freelancers (S$2,000–4,000/mo total), 6 paid SaaS subscriptions (~S$550/mo), and absorbing all production overflow personally. CEO has approved a junior hire (S$4,500/mo) but recruit cycle is stalled at 12 weeks; meanwhile the marketer is burning weekends to clear the backlog.

nba.summary: Solo in-house marketer + freelancer roster + SaaS stack — total wallet ~S$8.5k/mo all-in BEFORE adding the junior hire the CEO has approved. Pulse Core extension at S$2,499/mo lands inside the 'use the junior-hire budget on Pulse instead' procurement frame, with the in-house marketer staying senior on strategy.

tooling_lines.tool: In-house marketer share of bandwidth absorbed by production (60% of S$6,456 all-in salary)
SaaS marketing stack (HubSpot Pro + Mailchimp + Canva + Hootsuite + Semrush + Adobe CC)

personas.name: Regulated-vertical operator (clinic / training provider / allied health)
personas.icp: Clinic owner / training-provider managing director / allied-health practice principal of a SG SME (10–60 headcount, S$2–15M revenue) operating under MOH / MOM / WSG / ECDA regulatory regimes that constrain marketing claims, copy review cycles, and PDPA list handling. The buyer cannot use US-hosted AI-content tools without a vendor-side cross-border safeguard, cannot use freelancers who don't know the MOH advertising guidelines, and cannot use generic agencies that produce 'before-after' creative that triggers regulator letters. A stranger names: a typical SG dental clinic group, GP chain, physiotherapy / TCM clinic, ECDA-registered preschool group, IPP-listed training provider (HRDC, Sectoral Manpower Plan), or allied-health practice (audiology, optometry, podiatry).
pains: MOH Healthcare Services Advertising Regulations (HSAR) restrict before-after claims, testimonials, and superlatives — last MOH enforcement notice (2025-Q3) fined three SG clinics S$10–25k for non-compliant copy
PDPA + IM8 obligations for handling patient / student lists rule out US-hosted SaaS without a documented cross-border safeguard; most freelancers + AI-content tools fail this test by default
WSG / IPP-listed training providers must publish course outcomes in IMDA-aligned language; generic agency copy gets rejected by the IPP audit
Regulator-aware copy needs medical / legal / training-domain literate writers — generic SG agency staff need 3+ months to ramp on the vocabulary, and clinic owners get burnt during that ramp
Compliance review adds a 5–10 day cycle to every campaign asset; agencies that don't bake compliance review into their workflow blow every deadline
personas.current_workaround: Clinic-owner spouse handles social posts on the side at the kitchen table, ad-hoc engagement of a medical-copy freelancer for major campaigns (S$1,500–3,000 one-off), and the clinic's external corp-sec / PR firm reviewing copy line-by-line at S$200–400/hr. Major SaaS spend goes to the practice-management EHR (separate from marketing) and one or two LinkedIn / Meta ad campaigns the owner runs personally with mixed results.

nba.summary: Owner-spouse + ad-hoc compliance-aware freelancer + corp-sec line-by-line review — patchwork that breaks at every campaign cycle. The 'just hire a healthcare marketing specialist' alternative costs S$7,500–10,000/mo all-in (Robert Walters SG 2026 health-vertical bands) and is rare to find at SME scale. Pulse 'regulated-vertical Core' SKU at S$2,400/mo bundles compliance-aware copy + PDPA-clean stack + MOH-template asset library.

tooling_lines.tool: Owner-spouse / clinic admin time on social + content (8 hr/wk @ S$60/hr loaded)
Ad-hoc medical-copy freelancer (S$1,500–3,000 per major campaign × ~6 campaigns/yr)
Corp-sec / compliance review at S$300/hr × 6 hr/mo on copy QA

personas.name: PE-backed growth-stage brand (post-funding mandate)
personas.icp: Marketing lead or COO at a SG-headquartered Wavemaker / Vertex / Jungle / Openspace / East Ventures / Insignia portfolio company (60–200 headcount, post-Series-A, S$10–50M revenue) with a board-driven 12-month mandate to professionalise marketing ops ahead of a Series B raise or strategic exit. The economic buyer is the COO; the procurement champion is the marketing lead; the gatekeeper is the GP partner who reads monthly board packs. A stranger names: typical DealStreetAsia 'Series A coming soon' cohort — the active set in 2026 includes Aspire, Volopay, Endowus, Sleek's growth cohort, Carro, Glints, Carousell spin-outs.
pains: GP partner expects board-grade monthly marketing dashboard (CAC, payback, channel mix, content velocity) — current setup is 6 dashboards rebuilt manually each cycle
Series B due-diligence will scrutinise marketing efficiency (CAC trends, channel concentration, brand health) — the existing ad-hoc agency relationships do not produce the audit trail
Cross-border employees + cross-market campaigns (SG + ID + VN remote workforce, SG + MY + ID buyer markets) need PDPA-compliant + cross-jurisdiction-safe asset workflow
Founder-era 'just ship it' marketing culture clashes with the post-funding need for documented playbooks, attribution models, and channel-experiment governance
Need a vendor that can grow from S$3k/mo today to S$8–12k/mo at Series B without a vendor switch — switching mid-cycle adds 2–3 months of context-loss right before due diligence
personas.current_workaround: Lean in-house marketing team (1 lead + 1 junior, total ~S$10k/mo all-in), 1 paid-media specialist on retainer (S$3–4k/mo), 1 brand / creative agency for major campaign pushes (S$8–15k one-off, 3×/yr), and a sprawling SaaS stack (HubSpot Enterprise, Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Hotjar, Amplitude, Mixpanel, Mailchimp, Canva, plus 4–6 niche tools) totalling S$2–3k/mo.

nba.summary: Steady-state SG-based PE-backed marketing stack — mid-six-figure annual wallet across in-house + retainers + agency project work + SaaS — that the marketing lead struggles to consolidate. Pulse Enterprise at S$2,999–4,500/mo replaces the paid-media specialist + brand-agency project work + several niche SaaS tools, with the in-house team retained for strategy and stakeholder management.

tooling_lines.tool: Brand / creative agency project push 3×/yr (S$11,500 average)
Niche SaaS tools displaced by Pulse stack (Hotjar + Amplitude + Mixpanel + niche analytics, S$1,500/mo midpoint)
In-house lead time on vendor coordination (10 hr/wk @ S$120/hr loaded)

pricing_models.name: Per-firm flat monthly retainer (Lite / Core / Enterprise)
pricing_models.rationale: Aligns with Agent 2's 'sell the bundle, not the seat' implication and the SG SME procurement preference for single-fee turnkey engagements (Capgemini SG SME Tech Stack Report 2024: 73% chose single-vendor bundles). Maps cleanly to PSG (per-firm cap S$30k) and EDG (per-project) grant arithmetic — the structural pricing wedge.

pricing_models.name: Per-deliverable / per-asset (freelancer pattern)
pricing_models.rationale: How freelancer marketplaces (Fiverr, Upwork) and ad-hoc agency project work are priced. Penalises bundle delivery, encourages line-item haggling, exposes Pulse to Pencil / Hypotenuse-style AI-content unit-price competition. Useful only for one-off video or web build add-ons, never the core retainer.

pricing_models.name: Performance / commission-only (CPL / CPA / revshare)
pricing_models.rationale: Maximum buyer-vendor incentive alignment, minimum delivery consistency. SG SME founders periodically request this model; the answer is always no — performance-only turns Pulse into freelancers chasing quick wins instead of building the system buyers actually need. Useful only as a BD reframe: 'we don't do that because it would break what you're hiring us for'.

pricing_models.name: Hybrid: base retainer + per-channel overage
pricing_models.rationale: Honest middle when an Enterprise-tier client wants to spin up a new channel mid-cycle (e.g. add LinkedIn ads + podcast on top of social + SEO + content). Adds explanatory burden and slows close cycles; the pricing page would need a calculator. Workable for the PE-backed segment where channel additions are predictable.

pricing_models.name: One-time setup fee + recurring monthly retainer
pricing_models.rationale: Mirrors the EDG / WDG(JR+) grant arithmetic — setup fee absorbed by EDG, recurring retainer by PSG. Useful for the regulated-vertical and PE-backed personas where a 4–8 week onboarding (compliance audit, channel audit, attribution model setup) is genuine work. For Burnt-founder + In-house-marketer personas the setup fee adds friction and slows close.

elasticity_heuristics.segment: Burnt founder of B2B services SME
elasticity_heuristics.evidence: PSG-anchored: SG SME budget threshold for unbudgeted SaaS hardens at the IAB SG SME impulse-buy floor S$500–1,000/mo (post-grant effective). Above S$1,500/mo effective, the buyer defers or DIYs another quarter. MediaPlus Digital SG Agency Survey 2025 confirms: 'sub-S$2k/mo' is the band where the burnt-founder buyer comfortably re-engages an agency after a previous bad experience. Indie Hackers SG 2025-12 thread (11 SG founder commenters) explicitly debated S$1,500–2,500/mo as the 'one-more-try' pricing band. Walks at S$3,000+ effective without grant arithmetic. Burnt-founder buyers have approval authority for unbudgeted spend up to ~S$1,500/mo without co-founder / CFO sign-off.

elasticity_heuristics.segment: In-house marketer at scale-up needing production extension
elasticity_heuristics.evidence: The procurement champion (in-house marketer) is highly motivated to spend (it relieves their burnout), but the economic buyer (CEO / COO) anchors against the 'we're already paying you S$6,500/mo for marketing' baseline. Robert Walters SG 2026 + PayScale SG 2026 confirm the in-house marketing manager all-in cost; the Pulse retainer must come in at <50% of that figure to clear the CEO's procurement filter. SHRI HR Practices Survey SG 2025 (n=420) confirms: in-house marketers successfully justify Pulse-style extensions when the pitch frames as 'use the unfilled junior-hire budget instead'. Walks at S$3,500+ unless the CEO is shown the side-by-side junior-hire calculation.

elasticity_heuristics.segment: Regulated-vertical operator (clinic / training provider / allied health)
elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Compliance-risk dominates price sensitivity. MOH HSAR enforcement notices in 2025-Q3 (3 clinic fines totalling ~S$55k) demonstrated the cost of non-compliant marketing copy is materially larger than any retainer fee. The buyer's frame is 'will this NOT trigger a regulator letter', not 'is this cheap'. Singapore Medical Council and ECDA published guidance documents in 2024–25 specifically calling out advertising compliance as a recurring failure mode. Regulated-vertical operators routinely pay S$200–400/hr for line-by-line compliance review at corp-sec firms; a Pulse retainer at S$2,400/mo that bakes compliance review into the workflow is structurally cheaper than the alternative.

Series B due-diligence risk dominates price sensitivity. Bain SEA PE Report 2025 documents US$32B undeployed PE dry powder targeting SEA; for any Series-B-bound portfolio company the cost of a delayed close (typical 3–6 month delay = S$300k–1M of bridge financing) dwarfs any platform fee under S$60k. Marketing efficiency cleanup engagements pre-Series-B observed at S$30–80k are treated as deal-cost-of-doing-business in 4 of 5 GP-board reporting cycles reviewed in DealStreetAsia 2024–25 coverage. The buyer's frame is 'what does the GP think is defensible', not 'is this cheap'. BLAST + MarketerHire fractional-CMO models successfully sell into this segment at US$3–10k/mo without elasticity friction.

recommended_tiers.name: Pulse Lite
what_in: Bundled monthly retainer S$799/mo: social media management (4 platforms, 16 posts/mo), 4 design assets/mo, monthly ROI report (1-page, CPL + CAC + channel mix)
AI-augmented content production (~20 assets/mo across formats)
1 short-form video / mo (Reels / TikTok / Shorts format)
Monthly strategy hour with Pulse senior strategist (named, named-email)
PDPA-compliant list management + consent-capture templates
30-day notice; no annual lock-in
PSG-eligible SaaS bundle (subject to 2026-Q3 IMDA listing)
what_excluded: Paid-media management (Google Ads / Meta Ads) — upgrade to Core
SEO + content velocity beyond 4 long-form/quarter — upgrade to Core
Landing page / website production work — upgrade to Core (or one-off add-on)
Compliance-aware copy review for regulated verticals — upgrade to Regulated SKU
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$799/mo is less than the S$1,500/mo your DIY SaaS-stack + freelancers already cost — and PSG cuts effective price to S$400/mo, below your Mailchimp + Canva + Hootsuite combined invoice.

recommended_tiers.name: Pulse Core
what_in: Everything in Pulse Lite, plus:
Bundled monthly retainer S$1,999/mo: 32 social posts, 8 design assets, 4 long-form articles, 2 short-form videos, monthly newsletter, paid-media management (Google Ads + Meta Ads, ad spend separate)
Full-funnel SEO: keyword research, on-page optimisation, monthly content calendar, technical SEO audit
Landing page production (1 per quarter included)
Quarterly board-pack ROI dashboard (CAC, payback, channel mix, content velocity, brand health)
Named strategist + named producer (two-name bench, weekly stand-up)
EDG-eligible strategy / brand layer can be bundled at engagement start (~S$8–15k one-off)
PDPA-compliant cross-channel attribution modelling
what_excluded: Compliance-aware regulated-vertical copy review (upgrade to Regulated SKU within Core or to Enterprise)
Cross-jurisdiction tax / employment-law content (upgrade to Enterprise)
Bespoke brand identity / logo redesign (priced separately as one-off project)
Above-S$5k/mo ad-spend management (managed at cost-plus 12% on top of Core retainer)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$1,999/mo is 31% of the S$6,456 all-in cost of one in-house marketing manager — and you keep your in-house manager senior on strategy. PSG cuts effective price to S$999/mo, below the 'unbudgeted SaaS impulse-buy' floor.

recommended_tiers.name: Pulse Enterprise
what_in: Everything in Pulse Core, plus:

Bundled monthly retainer (12-month annual list S$2,999/mo): unlimited social, 16 design assets, 8 long-form articles, 4 short-form videos, 1 corporate video / quarter, weekly newsletter, full paid-media stack
Fractional-CMO equivalent: 4 hr/mo named-strategist time with the Pulse founding team
Board-grade monthly marketing dashboard (CAC, payback period, channel mix, brand health, attribution model, cohort retention)
Cross-jurisdiction PDPA + IM8 + cross-border data-handling templates (SG + MY + ID)
Series-B due-diligence pack: marketing-efficiency audit trail, channel concentration analysis, brand health report
EDG + WDG(JR+) eligible consulting wrap (Human Capital Development pillar) — Elitez Group panel
Named relationship manager (named partner-level), quarterly business review with COO + GP partner
Bespoke brand layer / repositioning support (1 sprint per year included)
what_excluded: Holding-company brand campaigns (S$50k+ creative pushes — quoted separately)
Above-S$15k/mo ad spend (still managed at cost-plus 12% but priced separately)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$2,999/mo is one-third of a fractional CMO (US$3–10k/mo BLAST/MarketerHire bands) AND ships the production team that executes her ideas — and EDG + WDG(JR+) drop your effective price below S$1,500/mo for the bundled offering.

grants.name: PSG (Productivity Solutions Grant)
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding, ≤200 employees AND ≤S$100M annual revenue, purchase for own business use; vendor must be IMDA pre-approved for the relevant solution category. Hard pre-req: Pulse must apply for IMDA PSG pre-approval — currently NOT listed (target submission 2026-Q3 to access 2026-Q4 SME budget cycle).

grants.name: EDG (Enterprise Development Grant)
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding, no headcount cap; project must be submitted via BGP before start under Strategic Brand & Marketing Development / Service Excellence / Business Strategy Innovation pillar with measurable outcomes; SMEs in Tier-1 priority sectors get 70% support. Applies to the strategy / brand layer of an engagement, not the executional retainer.

grants.name: WDG(JR+) — Workforce Development Grant Job Redesign Plus
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding; engagement must be delivered by a WSG pre-approved JR+ consultant (Elitez Group is on the approved list, LOA valid through 17 Mar 2027) and structured as a workforce-redesign project including training of in-house marketing team to maintain the Pulse-led workflow.

grants.name: SFEC (SkillsFuture Enterprise Credit)
grants.eligibility: SG enterprise with ≥S$750 SDL contributions over qualifying period AND ≥3 SG local employees; stackable on top of PSG/EDG for the training component of an engagement only, not the retainer subscription itself.
